title: Honey-Dijon Chicken Thighs
summary: Sweet, sticky, and ridiculously easy to make. These honey mustard chicken thighs are serious business.

method.description: In a large bowl, squeeze the juice of the lemon and whisk it together with the honey, Dijon mustard, paprika, and olive oil.
Add the chicken thighs to the bowl and toss until well coated. Season generously with salt and black pepper.
Arrange the chicken thighs in a single layer on a baking tray.
Roast for 30 mins, or until the skin is crispy and the chicken is cooked through.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges on the side.

tips: Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 4 days.
To freeze, allow the chicken to cool, then store in an airtight container for up to 3 months. Defrost in the fridge overnight and reheat in the oven until hot.
Works well for meal prep—pair with rice, roasted vegetables, or a fresh salad.
Swap chicken thighs for drumsticks or boneless thighs.
Use maple syrup instead of honey for a different sweetness.
Add smoked paprika or cayenne for a spicier kick.

chefs.name: Finn Tonry
chefs.summary: Finn Tonry (@Finntonry) is a chef and recipe developer who’s all about making healthy food exciting. After years working in some of London’s best restaurants, he’s now focused on creating high-protein dishes that look great, taste amazing, and actually make you feel good.
